theorem: Q in Z^3 on |x|^2=R => E_+(Q) <= (1+T'(Q))N^2 with T' <<_eps R^eps [DIV-CIRC]

claims.name: lemmaA_holds_on_lattice_shells
claims.detail: (E_+ <= (1+T')N^2 verified exactly on Z^3 shells R=101..9974: the proof check on real arithmetic data)

claims.name: Tprime_over_N_decreasing
claims.detail: (T'/N falls 0.107 -> 0.024 as R:101->9974, N:168->2040: T' grows sub-linearly => DR-2 holds for lattice shells, unlike the rich latitude circle where T'=N)

claims.name: Tprime_subpolynomial
claims.detail: (d logT'/d logR = 0.177 << 1; the per-point exponent logT'/logR falls 0.63->0.42, the signature of R^o(1))

claims.name: E_plus_is_O_Nsq
claims.detail: (E_+/N^2 <= 5.27 across all shells: lattice additive energy is O(N^2), DR-2 holds with room to spare -- the R^eps ceiling is never approached in practice)

claims.name: lattice_independent_mechanism
claims.detail: (R=1826: Z^3 and FCC give identical N=672, T'=24, E_+=1850400 -- the divisor mechanism is lattice-independent, so it applies to the BCC/FCC momentum shells of TECT)
